topics.definition: A system is a group of interrelated components working together toward a central objective. System Analysis determines WHAT the system must do before coding begins.
topics.elements: Input, Processor, Output, Control, Feedback, Environment, Boundaries
topics.characteristics: Organization, Interaction, Interdependence, Integration, Central Objective
topics.sdlc: Requirement Gathering -> System Analysis -> System Design -> Coding/Implementation -> Testing -> Deployment -> Maintenance
topics.modeling: Creating visual blueprints (Flowcharts, DFDs) of the architecture
topics.srs: Software Requirement Specification - formal document listing all functional and non-functional requirements
topics.srs_review: Critical meeting to finalize SRS with stakeholders before development

topics.waterfall: Strict, linear, sequential phases. Used for fixed-requirement projects. No going back once phase is done.
topics.prototype: Build dummy version -> Get Feedback -> Refine -> Build Final. Great for UI/UX clarity.
topics.spiral: Iterative loops focusing heavily on Risk Analysis. Used for large, critical systems. 4 phases: Planning, Risk Analysis, Engineering, Evaluation.
topics.winwin_spiral: Focuses on continuous negotiation between client and developer to meet budget and feature goals.
topics.prototyping: Visualizing features to extract accurate client needs
topics.data_dictionary: Master repository defining every data element (Metadata)
topics.dfd: Data Flow Diagram - Circles (Processes), Arrows (Data Flow), Rectangles (External Entities), Parallel Lines (Data Stores)

topics.definition: Translating SRS into technical blueprint. Answers HOW the system will be built.
topics.conceptual_design: High-Level: Focuses on UI/UX, user interaction, overall system architecture
topics.technical_design: Low-Level: Focuses on Database Schema, API endpoints, specific algorithms
topics.modular_design: Breaking software into independent components (Auth Module, Payment Module, Booking Module)
topics.cohesion: HIGH is GOOD - degree to which elements inside a single module belong together. One module = one job.
topics.coupling: LOW is GOOD - degree of interdependence between different modules. Failure in one should not crash another.

topics.objective: Delivering software on time, within budget, with required quality
topics.loc: Lines of Code - estimating size based on code lines (Outdated method)
topics.function_point: Estimating size based on number of features/functions (Modern method)
topics.cocomo: Constructive Cost Model - mathematical formula for Effort (Person-Months) and Cost estimation
topics.cocomo_categories: Organic (Small/Simple), Semi-Detached (Medium like JaldiRide), Embedded (Highly Complex/Mission Critical)
topics.risk_analysis: Identification -> Projection (Probability & Impact) -> Management (Plan B) -> Monitoring
topics.scheduling: Using Gantt Charts to map tasks to timelines

topics.verification: Are we building the product RIGHT? Checking code/design against SRS during development.
topics.validation: Are we building the RIGHT product? Checking if software solves user's real-world problem.
topics.unit_testing: Testing smallest individual function/component in isolation
topics.integration_testing: Combining modules and testing communication (Top-Down or Bottom-Up)
topics.system_testing: Testing fully integrated software including Performance (Load) and Security testing
topics.oo_testing: Object-Oriented Testing - focuses on Encapsulation and Inheritance testing
topics.alpha_testing: Conducted internally by developers/QA in controlled environment
topics.beta_testing: Conducted by small group of real external users in real-world conditions before launch
